name: The Bouconne Forest - trail loop
name_original: La forêt de Bouconne – boucle trail
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lévignac traverse a landscape characterized by extensive woodlands, notably the Bouconne Forest, and scenic waterways like the Save River. The region offers diverse terrain, from well-maintained forest trails to paths along riverbanks. While generally gentle, some routes include moderate elevation changes, providing varied running experiences. Best jogging routes around Lévignac The most popular jogging route is The Bouconne Forest - trail loop, an 8.4 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This route explores the heart of the Bouconne Forest, offering a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local runners is Bordette Lake loop from Forêt Domaniale de Bouconne, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This route circles the tranquil Bordette Lake within the Bouconne Forest, featuring shaded paths. Local runners also love the Titi's race loop from Borne en pierre, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through varied forest terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Lévignac is defined by extensive woodlands, scenic waterways, and gentle hills, with routes primarily within the Bouconne Forest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Lévignac are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Lévignac's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Lévignac?
A: There are over 60 running routes around Lévignac, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences. These routes primarily explore the extensive woodlands and scenic waterways of the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Lévignac?
A: The running trails in Lévignac are characterized by extensive woodlands, especially within the &lt;strong&gt;Bouconne Forest&lt;/strong&gt;, and scenic paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Save River&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll find diverse terrain, from well-maintained forest trails to gentler riverbank paths. While generally mild, some routes do include moderate elevation changes, providing varied running experiences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Lévignac offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. Out of the 62 available routes, 7 are specifically rated as easy. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and less elevation gain.

Q: Can I find challenging running routes around Lévignac?
A: For more experienced runners seeking a challenge, Lévignac has 6 routes rated as difficult. These trails often feature more significant elevation changes and varied terrain, providing a more demanding workout. The annual 'FOREST RUN Lévignac' event also highlights the area's suitability for longer, more challenging trail runs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Lévignac?
A: Many of the running routes in Lévignac are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974174578/the-bouconne-forest-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bouconne Forest - trail loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 13.6 km circular route that explores the heart of the forest.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or points of interest along the running trails?
A: The region boasts several scenic highlights. Within the Bouconne Forest, you can run around the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordette Lake&lt;/a&gt;, which offers shaded paths and a refreshing natural setting. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/155744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Castéra&lt;/a&gt;, providing spectacular vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are the running routes in Lévignac suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the routes, especially the easier ones within the Bouconne Forest, are suitable for families. The diverse trails offer options for various ages and fitness levels, allowing families to enjoy a run or walk together in nature. The shaded paths around Bordette Lake are particularly pleasant for family outings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas like the Bouconne Forest, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash and adhere to local regulations, especially concerning wildlife protection. Always check for specific signage at trailheads.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Lévignac?
A: The running routes in Lévignac are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have explored the area using komoot, often praising the quiet woodlands, well-maintained paths, and the natural beauty of the Bouconne Forest.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at key access points to the Bouconne Forest and other popular trailheads around Lévignac. Many routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10620068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordette Lake loop from Forêt Domaniale de Bouconne&lt;/a&gt;, start from areas with designated parking.

Q: Are there any running events or races held in Lévignac?
A: Yes, Lévignac is known for the annual 'FOREST RUN Lévignac,' a popular trail race that offers 13km and 23km options. This event highlights the excellent running conditions and scenic beauty of the Bouconne Forest, attracting runners from the region.

Q: What is a good route for a moderate run in the Bouconne Forest?
A: A great option for a moderate run is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10620092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Titi's race loop from Borne en pierre&lt;/a&gt;. This 10 km trail leads through varied forest terrain and is typically completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes, offering a good balance of distance and challenge.
tips: itinerary taking exactly the route proposed by the Bouconne leisure center. Shaded and accessible, we meet animals like deer or hares there regularly.
20min west of Toulouse, the forest of Bouconne is the ideal place to escape. Many facilities allow you to enjoy this natural space.

A real curiosity of the Bouconne forest, the Chappe Telegraph Tower is a tower dating from 1834. It was used for the transmission of messages by visual communication between semaphores distributed over a network traversing France.
